0.question: What is Mandalart?
0.answer: Mandalart is a thinking expansion tool developed by Japanese designer Hiroaki Imaizumi in 1987. It breaks a central goal into 8 key elements, and each element into 8 detailed actions, creating a total of 81 squares of action plans.

1.question: Is the Mandalart planner free?
1.answer: Yes, it's completely free. You can start using it without signing up. Sign in to sync your data across multiple devices.

2.question: Can I save my Mandalart?
2.answer: In guest mode, it's automatically saved in your browser. Sign in with a Google account to save it securely in the cloud.

3.question: How many squares does a Mandalart have?
3.answer: 81 squares in total. It consists of 1 center square (core goal) + 8 squares (key elements) + 72 squares (detailed action plans).

4.question: What does Shohei Ohtani's Mandalart look like?
4.answer: Shohei Ohtani created his Mandalart as a first-year high school student with 'First pick in the draft by 8 teams' as his central goal. He set 8 key elements including physical strength, mental toughness, pitching power, and control, with detailed action plans for each.

cta.title: Create Your Own Mandalart Now
cta.subtitle: Start right away without signing in. Completely free.

seo.title: What is Mandalart? — The Ultimate Thinking Expansion Tool for Achieving Goals
seo.description: Discover the origin, structure, and methods of Mandalart. Learn how to turn your goals into concrete action plans with 81 squares.

header.title: What is Mandalart?
header.subtitle: The ultimate thinking expansion tool for achieving goals

history.title: Origin and History of Mandalart
history.paragraph1: Mandalart is a thinking expansion tool developed by Japanese designer Hiroaki Imaizumi (今泉浩晃) in {{year}}. It originated from the symmetrical structure inspired by the Buddhist Mandala (曼荼羅).
history.paragraph1Strong: Japanese designer Hiroaki Imaizumi (今泉浩晃) in 1987
history.paragraph2: The name itself is a compound of three words.
history.paragraph2Suffix: — it embodies the idea of elevating the skill of achieving goals into an art form.
history.paragraph3: Like the radial symmetry of a Mandala, Mandalart starts from a central goal and expands thinking outward in a balanced way. This structural characteristic naturally helps the brain perform association and divergent thinking, similar to a Mandala.

structure.title: The Structure of Mandalart — The Secret of 81 Squares
structure.intro: Mandalart consists of a 9×9 = 81-square grid. These 81 squares form an elaborate three-level structure.

card0.label: Core Goal
card0.description: The very center of the 9×9 grid

structure.centerMatrix: The center square is the core goal, and the 8 surrounding squares are the key elements.
structure.outerMatrix: Each key element occupies the center of its own 3×3 block, with the surrounding 8 squares becoming specific action items.
structure.summaryStrong: 1 core goal + 8 key elements + 64 action items = 73 meaningful squares
structure.summarySuffix: are completed. (The remaining 8 squares are structurally empty corner blocks.)

howto.title: How to Create a Mandalart — 5-Step Guide

0.title: Set Your Central Goal
0.body: Apply the SMART principle. Write a Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound goal in the center square.

1.title: Identify 8 Key Elements
1.body: Brainstorm 8 essential categories needed to achieve your goal. This step is the core of Mandalart. Write down everything that comes to mind, then select the 8 most important ones.

2.title: Write Detailed Actions
2.body: For each key element, create 8 specific action plans. Make them concrete enough to actually execute, like "Walk 30 minutes every day."

3.title: Set Priorities
3.body: Start with the most important and feasible among the 64 action items. You don't need to fill all squares at once.

4.title: Execute and Review
4.body: Regularly check your progress and mark completed items. Don't hesitate to revise when circumstances change. A Mandalart should be a living plan.

comparisonTable.title: Mandalart vs Other Goal-Setting Methods
comparisonTable.description: Among various goal-setting methods, Mandalart stands out in terms of specificity and comprehensive overview.

ohtani.title: Shohei Ohtani's Mandalart
ohtani.intro: The most famous example that brought Mandalart to worldwide attention is Shohei Ohtani. He created his Mandalart as a first-year student at Hanamaki Higashi High School.
ohtani.goalText: First pick in the draft by 8 teams
ohtani.conclusion: Ohtani has indeed achieved historic success in MLB. What's particularly noteworthy is the key element of "Luck." He treated even luck as something controllable, and the fact that its action items focused on
ohtani.conclusionSuffix: shows the depth of the Mandalart approach.
